eia_inventory.summary: EIA reported a draw of 8.0 million barrels in U.S. commercial crude oil inventories (excluding SPR) for the week ending May 29, 2026, the largest weekly draw since February 2026.

price_momentum.summary: WTI crude oil is trading at approximately $89.71/bbl as of June 10, 2026, down roughly 15% from ~$106/bbl four weeks ago, driven by ceasefire optimism and partial easing of Strait of Hormuz disruption

industrials.oneliner: Industrials momentum is broadly bullish — surging PMI, freight volumes, and equities offset a near-term capex dip.

new_orders.summary: The ISM Manufacturing New Orders sub-index registered 56.8 in May 2026, up 2.7 points from April's reading of 54.1, marking its fifth consecutive month of expansion.

manufacturing_pmi.summary: The ISM Manufacturing PMI rose to 54.0 in May 2026, up from 52.7 in each of the prior two months, beating forecasts of 53.0 (source: tradingeconomics.com, reported June 2026).

asx_industrials.summary: ASX 200 Industrials (XNJ) closed at 8,358.60 on 10 Jun 2026, up approximately +4.5% from ~8,002 in mid-May 2026, exceeding the +3% threshold.

china_pmi.summary: The Caixin China Manufacturing PMI came in at 51.8 in May 2026, beating the consensus forecast of 51.4 and marking six consecutive months above the 50-point expansion threshold.

copper_price.summary: LME copper is trading at approximately $13,845/tonne (≈$6.28/lb) as of June 10, 2026, down ~2.06% over the past 30 days — within the flat ±3% band.
